bio.title: SSR Web Developer
bio.subtitle: I create aesthetically pleasing and functional solutions through code and design. Passionate about continuous learning, tackling new challenges, and exploring solo entrepreneurship. Currently earning my programming degree while expanding my skills in modern web & app technologies.

sectionOne.title: About me
texts: Hi! I'm Benja, a full-stack developer who loves technology and design. I'm Argentinian, I live in Buenos Aires, and I'm 24 years old. If you took the time to explore and create your account, I sincerely appreciate it. Now I can spam your email until you hire me... nah, just kidding 👀. You can now tweet whatever you want and interact with what I built. Would you say something nice about my projects? I'd really appreciate it. This journey in programming has just begun and i'd love to guide you through the process, but first lets talk about me.

I didn't follow the traditional path. After finishing high school in 2025, I enrolled at the University of Buenos Aires to study Biochemistry. But after two years in the program, I realized that I was NOT going to be the next up-and-coming Walter White... more like down-and-retreating. No? Hello?

While studying biochemistry, I started working at my grandfather's office as an accounting assistant, which was repetitive and monotonous work. I didn't like it, but it gave me money and allowed me to pay for my hobbies. What did I learn from this stage? That I hate routines and monotony. I like being in a dynamic environment where I am constantly challenged, forced to learn new things and improve every day. In an place where I can grow — something my grandfather's accounting studio would have never allowed me to do.

When I dropped out of college I realized I wasn't heading towards the direction I wanted. I felt frustrated and with no purpose. So I decided to take a different path and got into programming. And what an excellent decision. At first, it was difficult to balance work and being a self-taught programer. But my passion for solving errors and learning new technologies pushed me to get out of ruts faster by the day. Nowadays, I feel very happy that I took this path. I feel challenged every day.

Before I spread my wings and dove into programming 100%, I took on freelance projects to get my feet wet. I've learned a lot from them, and I'm still learning. Specially because I got to experience both sides of the coin: what its like to lead people that have less experience, and learning from the masters of the craft. I've made some great friends and connections that I'm very thankful for. Programming isnt just about coding, its about learning, networking, and building great relationships.

Oh yeah, so this is me. In my free time, I like to make electronic music. Yes, scoliosis and shoulder pain till death for me thanks. Specifically, for those who know the scene (real ones, word), I make peak-time techno, a genre that is quite popular nowadays in the world — the one outside my room I've been told about. So yeah, I'm quite the stay-at-home nerd, but I promise I have friends and a girlfriend (at least as of now). But if I didn't have any loved ones you just know I would be straight crushing it in Assembly by now.
So anyways, the pictures below are from a photoshoot I invested in because you can't be a techno artist without going for the casual, kinda-gothic, and cringy Insta feed.
artistSection.myMusicTitleTexts: If you're an electronic music fan, I encourage you to listen to these bangers! Any feedback would be wonderfull. I hope you enjoy my tunes!

texts.randomTalent: Picking up things with my feet. Don't ask me how I discovered it, but it's a talent that has come in handy on more than one occasion.
texts.name: Benjamin Henley Saubidet
texts.favouriteThing: Spamming W's in the group chat.
texts.leastFavouriteThing: Getting hate for spamming W's in the group chat.
texts.hobbies: Producing music, Working out, Rocking out, meditation, park-time with the doggies, raving, DJ'ing in cool clubs, binge watching youtube shorts until unconscious, PARKOUR, learning new things, traveling (still planning my world tour) and connecting with people.
texts.feats: I bring random friend groups together. Open communication is a must for me, even when it means having tough conversations. I'm always looking to push boundaries, pick up new skills, and refine the ones I already have. Drawing is a big part of my life—my room walls are covered in my artwork (don't worry, my mental health is fine). I'm as Argentine as it gets: I live for asados and mate, and I hate our economy.
texts.coffee: Im having two coffees a day, don't judge. I'm a coffee lover, not a coffee snob... but I have to admit I like Starbucks. I also drink mate, a traditional Argentine drink.

techStack.title: Tech Stack
techStack.description: I work with a modern and efficient tech stack, combining front-end, back-end, databases, and cloud technologies to build scalable applications.

softSkills.title: Soft Skills
softSkills.description: Every project is an opportunity to grow, innovate, and refine my expertise. Below is a glimpse into the skills that shape my work and drive my passion for technology.

texts.title: Technical Skills
texts.description: I'm a full-stack developer specializing in React, Next.js, and Nest.js with GraphQL and Apollo Client. I work with PostgreSQL, Firebase Firestore, and enjoy styling with Tailwind CSS. I have experience with Docker for scalable deployments. Currently, I'm learning React Native and Swift for native app development. I'm also digging into AWS for cloud services and serverless architecture.

texts.title: Bilingual Experience & UI/UX
texts.description: Fun fact: my father is English, so I am bilingual. These skills in English and Spanish have allowed me to collaborate efficiently in diverse teams, and with numerous clients. improving internationalization, UI/UX, and accessibility.

texts.title: Project Collaboration
texts.description: I enjoy working toward a team vision. At ibijetrent, I collaborated with a frontend developer to integrate complex features into a jet ski reservation platform, delivering a solution that exceeded expectations. At Encender, I work with a team of developers to build and integrate iframes into the main Salesforce site.

texts.title: Database & API Experience
texts.description: I've built and optimized SQL (PostgreSQL) and NoSQL (Firebase) databases for scalable applications, implementing efficient CRUD operations and designing GraphQL APIs with Apollo Client for seamless data fetching. This web page's backend is built with Node.js and Nest.js, with tweet data retrieved from a PostgreSQL database. I also containerized the application using Docker, ensuring a consistent and portable development environment.

texts.title: Problem-Solving
texts.description: I enjoy breaking down complex problems and applying design principles and best development practices to find efficient solutions. Analyzing a problem from multiple angles allows me to deliver innovative and well-structured results. When designing a project's architecture, I prioritize scalability, maintainability, and performance to ensure long-term efficiency. If this means learning a new technology, to update existing infrastructure so be it.

texts.title: Creativity & Innovation
texts.description: In my programming career, creativity and innovation are at the core of everything I build. I constantly explore new technologies and frameworks, blending them in unique ways to develop efficient and visually engaging solutions. Whether designing complex algorithms or intuitive user interfaces, I focus on thinking outside the box to solve problems and enhance user experiences. I enjoy experimenting with fresh ideas, pushing boundaries, and turning challenges into opportunities for growth. This mindset allows me to create impactful projects that not only meet technical requirements but also bring a creative spark to the digital space.

texts.title: Adaptability & Continuous Learning
texts.description: I'm always exploring new technologies and methodologies to enhance my skills and stay ahead of industry trends. I'm currently learning about native app and exploring new frameworks, together with libraries to improve both my frontend and backend coding capabilities.

texts.title: Communication & Teamwork
texts.description: I have experience collaborating with multidisciplinary teams, facilitating effective communication, and ensuring smooth project progress. I'm comfortable working with clients to understand their needs and deliver solutions that meet their expectations.

texts.title: Time Management & Organization
texts.description: I excel at managing multiple tasks and prioritizing effectively to ensure timely, high-quality project deliveries. I have a strong work ethic and believe that the best way to learn is through hands-on experience—this mindset has shaped my programming journey. My first freelance projects taught me the importance of deadlines and delivering quality work on time. At Encender, I am adapting to a new way of working, fast-paced environment, working with multiple clients and projects, which has strengthened my ability to work dynamically and efficiently.

achievements.description: Key metrics and accomplishments from my professional journey

clientSatisfaction.description: Consistently delivered high-quality solutions that exceed client expectations

education.title: Education & Training
education.description: Completed APX course on Full Stack Development and Udemy courses on Mobile Development. Now i'm going for a degree in Computer science at the Technological University of Buenos Aires (UTN).

careerMilestone.title: Career Milestone
careerMilestone.description: Landed my first company job at Encender after months of hard work and dedication
